steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is a past tense verb. This word is connected to بِعَذَابٍ وَاقِعٍ (about a falling punishment) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb. This word connects to بِعَذَابٍ (about punishment) in the ayah.
The verb 'he asked' changes to 'one who asks' by adding an alif (ا) after the first letter. This makes it into the person doing the asking.
The word changed to show it's 'an asker' (not a specific one) by adding the tanween (double marks) at the end. It's doing the asking action in this sentence.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The verb 'he punished' becomes the noun 'punishment' by adding an alif in the middle. Like changing 'hit' to 'hitting'.

steps.m: with/by a punishment (connected to سَأَلَ - he asked)
steps.n: The letter ب (meaning 'with/by') was added to the start, showing this punishment is what was asked about
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed to active participle form to describe the punishment (عَذَابٍ) as something that is occurring
Added tanwin kasra to match the case of عَذَابٍ that it describes

steps.m: to reject or hide the truth

steps.n: Changed to active participle form to show who does the action
The ين ending makes it plural for masculine beings. The word is in genitive case because it comes after the preposition ل (for/to).
Added لِ prefix to show direction/purpose ('for')

steps.m: for the disbelievers (who will face a punishment)
steps.n: Added emphasis with shadda on ل and added final vowel. The word relates to the following phrase which mentions their punishment.
This is a special verb used for negation in Arabic. It's irregular and only appears in past tense form but gives present meaning.
This special word is used to say something does not exist or is not true. Here it's saying there is not any preventer/defender (دَافِعٌ) of the punishment.
This is a preposition particle showing possession or purpose
Added هُۥ (him) to show who the preposition refers to. This word connects to دَافِعٌ (defender/preventer) in the ayah, showing there is no defender for him.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show who does the action. This word is the subject of the negative verb ليس (there is not).

steps.m: a repeller/one who repels (subject of لَيْسَ)
steps.n: Added double damma to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a repeller' instead of 'the repeller')
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word اللَّهِ (Allah)
Added shadda for emphasis and smooth pronunciation
Changed to the special word for Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that always refers to the One True God in Islam.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, shadda on ل, and changed initial alif. This word is possessed by مِّنَ (from) in this ayah.
This word shows that something belongs to or has a certain quality
Added kasra (ِ) and changed و to ى to show this word is being used to describe what comes after it. Here it's describing Allah as the owner of something.
The root describes upward movement
Forms a noun indicating the location where ascending happens
Makes the word plural to show multiple places of ascending

steps.m: the ways of ascending (possessed by ذِى)
steps.n: The kasra ending shows this word belongs to or is possessed by ذِى (owner of) that comes before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changes 'he ascended' to 'they ascend' by adding تَ at start - used for angels going up
Adding the vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
This is not correct - 'malaa'ik' (angels) comes from 'malak' (angel), not from 'malaka' (to possess). They are different root words.
Added feminine ending to show this refers to the whole group of angels together
Added 'the' to specify these particular angels
Changed to standard Quranic spelling and added U ending to show angels are the ones doing the ascending
Added damma (u sound) to first letter and joined root letters to make the word for spirit
Added ٱلْ to make the word definite. The ر gets a shadda due to assimilation with the ل of the definite article.
Added وَ (and) at the beginning and ُ at the end to show it's the subject doing the action of ascending (تَعْرُجُ) along with the angels (ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةُ)
This is a basic preposition showing direction or movement toward something. In this ayah, it shows where the angels and spirit are ascending to.

steps.m: to Him (referring to Allah, who the angels and spirit ascend to)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show this means 'to Him'. The ي is added before هِ to connect the sounds smoothly. This word shows the destination of the angels and spirit's ascension.
Basic preposition showing location or being inside something
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. In this ayah, it shows the angels and spirit ascending within a specific day.
Connected the letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun. The و becomes silent with sukun.
Added tanween kasra to show this word is indefinite (a day) and is possessed by something (specifically مِقْدَارُهُۥ - its measure)
Added fathas to make it past tense. This is the theoretical form before the weak letter change.
The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter between two fathas

steps.m: to measure or determine an amount

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make a noun meaning 'amount' or 'measure'
Added ا after middle letter to make a word meaning 'the act/process of measuring'
Added مِ prefix to create noun indicating instrument/means of measuring
Added ُ to show this word is doing the action of كَانَ (was)
Added هُۥ to show possession - 'its measure'
Connected the root letters with basic vowel marks
Added ين to make it into fifty. This number describes how long something is (its measure/amount)

steps.m: to join together in a large number

steps.n: Added vowel marks to create the noun meaning 'thousand'. This word is counting the number of years (سَنَةٍ) in this ayah.
Added fatha at the end because this number is being counted (like saying 'one thousand years')
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic word structure
Changed final و to ة to create a noun meaning one complete cycle of time

steps.m: a year (possessed by خَمْسِينَ أَلْفَ - fifty thousand)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this word is indefinite (a year) and is possessed by the number fifty thousand in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he was patient' to 'be patient!' by adding alif at start and changing vowels - this makes it a command

steps.m: so be patient! (connected to صَبْرًا جَمِيلًا - with beautiful patience)
steps.n: The فَ shows this command is connected to what follows it (صَبْرًا جَمِيلًا - with beautiful patience)
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it into a noun meaning the act of being patient

steps.m: patience (as the object of فَٱصْبِرْ - have patience)
steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) at the end to show this word is the object being acted upon in the sentence. It's what kind of patience is being commanded in فَٱصْبِرْ (have patience)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Forms an adjective describing the quality of beauty
This word describes how the patience (صَبْرًا) should be done. The ًا ending shows it's being used as an adverb to describe the action.
This is a special word that adds emphasis, like saying 'definitely' or 'for sure'

steps.m: indeed they (emphasizes يَرَوْنَهُ - they see it)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show we're talking about a group of people. This word connects to يَرَوْنَهُ (they see it) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The word changes from past tense 'saw' to present tense 'sees', and the ending changes to a long alif (ى) because it's a special verb ending in a weak letter
Changes from 'he sees' to 'they see' by adding ون at the end (shortened to just ن because it's present tense)
Added هُ to show who is being seen. This connects to إِنَّهُمْ (indeed they) in the ayah, showing what they are seeing.
This root describes distance or remoteness
Added ي in the middle and vowel marks to make it into a describing word
The ًا at the end shows this word is receiving the action of seeing - they see IT as distant
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The ي changes to ى at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added نَ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action, and changed to present tense
Added هُ at the end to show who is being seen. Also added the alif (ٰ) mark to show the long vowel sound.
Added وَ at the start to connect this word with what comes before it
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb showing the basic state of being near
Changed the verb into an adjective describing the quality of being near

steps.m: near (object of نَرَىٰهُ - we see it)
steps.n: Added tanwin alif to show this word is being acted upon by the verb نَرَىٰهُ (we see it)
Connected the root letters with fatha and sukun to make the basic noun form.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is in accusative case
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
تَ at start shows it's either 'she' or 'you' doing the action in present time

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Connected the root letters س-م-و with vowels to make 'he/it rose up' in the past
Added ء at the end to make it a noun meaning the thing that is high up (the sky)

steps.m: the sky (subject of تَكُونُ - it becomes)
steps.n: Added ٱل to make it specific ('the sky'), doubled the س to show it joins with ٱل, and added ُ to show it's doing the action in تَكُونُ (becomes)

steps.m: to melt slowly, to give time

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun describing what results from melting
Added ال to specify this particular molten substance, and ِ because it follows كَ (like)

steps.m: like the molten substance (describing how the sky will look)
steps.n: The كَ shows this is comparing something to molten metal. In this ayah, it's describing how the sky will look on that day.
Added damma vowels to connect the letters and make it a present tense verb base
Added تَ at the beginning to show the mountains (feminine) are doing the action
Adding وَ at the start means 'and'
Root letters put together with vowels to make the noun 'mountain'
Changes the word to show many mountains instead of just one
The ُ shows this word is doing the action in تَكُونُ (will become)
ٱلْ makes it refer to specific mountains that are known

steps.m: wool, especially colored or processed wool

steps.n: Root letters formed into a noun meaning colored wool
The kasra shows this wool belongs to or follows something else
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to specific wool that's being discussed, not just any wool

steps.m: like the wool (describing how the mountains will become) (subject of تَكُونُ)
steps.n: Adding كَ shows the mountains will become similar to wool. This word describes the state the mountains (الْجِبَالُ) will be in, as mentioned in the verb تَكُونُ (will become).
This is a negative particle that prevents something from happening. In this ayah, it stops the action of asking/inquiring between close friends.

The و connects this negation to what came before, showing this is an additional thing that won't happen. In this ayah, it indicates another aspect of that difficult day - that even close friends won't ask about each other.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb
Added يَ at the start to show this is happening now. Changed vowels to show ongoing action. This word shows no close friend asks about another close friend in this ayah.

steps.m: one who is very close/intimate (like family)
steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters to show this describes someone with this strong quality. Like how we say someone is 'warm-hearted' in English to mean they are caring and close.

steps.m: a close intimate one (subject of يَسْـَٔلُ - asks about)
steps.n: The ٌ shows this is indefinite (like saying 'a' in English) and is the subject doing the asking in the verse

steps.m: close friend (from the warmth of friendship)
steps.n: The root means 'heat/warmth' and by adding ي in the middle, it becomes 'someone warm/close' - like a close friend who gives you warm feelings

steps.m: a close friend (object of يَسْـَٔلُ/asks about)
steps.n: The tanween (ًا) shows this is indefinite (a/an) and is the object being asked about in the phrase 'and no close friend asks about [another] close friend'
Adding shadda doubles the middle letter ص to make the meaning more intense - like making something extra clear or visible
Adding يُ at the start changes it from 'making someone see' to 'being made to see' - like changing from active to passive voice
Adding ون changes it from one person to many people being shown

steps.m: they are made to see them (the sinners will see their loved ones but can't help them)
steps.n: Adding هم shows who they are made to see. In this verse, it refers to the sinners being made to see their loved ones on the Day of Judgment, but being unable to ransom themselves with them.
Adding fathas makes this a past tense verb meaning 'he wished'
Adding ي changes it from past to present tense
Adding ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence
Doubling the last letter makes the meaning stronger/more intense
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: one who commits crimes (doer of يَوَدُّ - wishes)
steps.n: Adding مُ at the start and changing the middle vowel to ِ turns a crime/sin into a person who commits crimes. Like turning 'steal' into 'thief'. This person is doing the wishing in this verse.
Adding ال (the) makes it 'the criminal' instead of just 'a criminal', and the ُ ending shows this criminal is doing something in the sentence

steps.m: if (introduces an impossible wish in يَوَدُّ/wishes)
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces impossible wishes or hypothetical situations that can't happen. In this ayah, it connects to يَوَدُّ (wishes) to show what the criminal wishes could happen, even though it's impossible.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
The pattern means doing the action to oneself

steps.m: he redeems himself (wishes to do in this ayah)
steps.n: Changes to present tense and shows what the person wishes to do in this context

This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects the word عَذَابِ (punishment) to the verb يَفْتَدِى (ransom), showing what the criminal wishes to ransom himself from.
Added the alif after the second letter and vowel marks to make it a noun meaning the result of punishment
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to يَوْمِئِذٍ (that day), forming the phrase 'punishment of that day'
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun 'day'
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added ئِذٍۭ to specify 'that specific day' being referred to in the context
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
These are two different words - 'banaa' means 'he built' while 'bani' means 'sons of'. They are not related by a transformation.
Added هِ at the end to show possession - these are his sons
Added بِ at beginning to show these sons are being used as a means of ransom
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to accompany'
Added alif after first letter to make it mean 'one who does the action'
Added ة to make it refer to a female companion
Added هِ to show possession ('his'). The ة changes to ت before adding هِ.
Added وَ at beginning to mean 'and'. The alif is written as ٰ (long alif) in this form.
Connected the letters together and added fatha to make the basic word for 'brother'
Added kasra to prepare for possessive 'my'
Added yaa to make it mean 'my brother'
Added هِ (hi) at the end to change from 'my brother' to 'his brother' - shows who owns/has the brother
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: to separate or distinguish between things

steps.n: Added vowels to make it 'he separated' in the past tense
Changes from a verb (he separated) to a noun (a separated group)
Added ة to make it a specific unit or group
Added connecting word 'and'

steps.m: and his family unit (that shelters him)
steps.n: Added possession ('his') and shows connection to the following word تُـْٔوِيهِ (shelters him)
This is a special word (relative pronoun) used to connect descriptions to feminine things or people. It's like saying 'that' or 'which' when talking about something feminine.
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one that' instead of just 'that'

steps.m: which (referring to فَصِيلَتِهِ - his closest relatives)
steps.n: Final spelling changes: 1) ا becomes ٱ at start, 2) ل gets shadda (ّ), 3) ي becomes ى at end. This word connects to فَصِيلَتِهِ (his closest relatives) to tell us more about them.
Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ) to mean 'he sheltered'
Changed from past to present tense and made it feminine by adding تُ at start
Added ه at the end to show who receives the shelter/protection
This is a word that refers to any person or people in general. In this ayah, it's talking about whoever will be saved.
The وَ connects this to what came before it, adding the meaning of 'and'
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it shows that something is 'in the earth'.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it pronounceable

steps.m: the earth (possessed by/belonging to something mentioned after)
steps.n: Added ال (the) to make it specific, and the kasra ( ِ ) shows it belongs to something after it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from 'gathered' action to 'all together' by adding ي to make it describe a complete group
Adding ًا (an) at the end turns this word into an adverb that describes how something happens - in this case, it shows everyone in the earth is together as one group
This is the basic form of the word that connects events in order, showing that one thing happens after another
Dots and marks added to show this word means 'then' and strongly connects two actions (like a bridge between what happened before and after)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added alif at beginning to show causing someone else to be saved
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added هِ to show who is being saved
This is a particle of emphatic negation or rejection. It's stronger than just saying 'no' - it's like saying 'absolutely not!' or 'by no means!'
Adding ا at the end makes the word stronger, like adding 'indeed' in English

steps.m: no indeed! (strongly rejecting what came before إِنَّهَا لَظَىٰ)
steps.n: The آ is just a writing variation of ا that shows the sound is stretched when reading. This word is used to strongly reject something - here it's rejecting what came before the next phrase 'Indeed, it is the Flame (of Hell)'
This is a particle that adds emphasis to لَظَىٰ (blazing fire) in this ayah
Added ها which refers back to لَظَىٰ (the blazing fire) mentioned later in the ayah. The ها is feminine because لَظَىٰ is a feminine word.
This word comes from letters that convey the meaning of an intense, blazing flame. It's used as a proper noun for a specific level of Hell.
Added fatha marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly

steps.m: Blazing Fire (as a proper name for a level of Hell)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٰ) to show this is the complete proper noun form. This word specifically names one of the levels of Hell known for its intense flames.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who pulls out repeatedly and forcefully
steps.n: The doubled ز (shadda) shows intensity, and the ا shows this is someone's regular action or job

steps.m: female one who pulls out repeatedly
steps.n: Adding ة makes this describe a female instead of male

steps.m: a female who pulls out repeatedly and forcefully (describes جَهَنَّمَ/Hell)
steps.n: The tanween (ً) shows this is describing Hell (جَهَنَّمَ) in this ayah, which pulls out/removes things forcefully
Adding vowel marks to make a basic past tense verb
These are actually two different words - not a transformation. الشَّوَى means skin/limbs/extremities while شَوَى means to roast/grill.
The لِّ adds emphasis and means 'surely for'
The ى at the end shows this is a noun meaning 'extremities' (like hands and feet)
The fathas show this is a basic past tense verb meaning 'he called'
Added ت at beginning to make it 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added silent alif at end to show multiple people doing the action
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to people in a general way. In this ayah, it's the object of تَدْعُوا۟ (you call), referring to whoever the caller invites.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he turned away completely (connected to وَتَوَلَّىٰ - and turned away)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the start makes the action more complete and emphatic. This word pairs with وَتَوَلَّىٰ (and turned away) to emphasize the complete turning away.

steps.m: to be close to or follow something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and doubled ل to show someone actively turning themselves away from something
Added و at beginning to connect this action with what came before it

steps.m: to gather or collect things together

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word appears with فَأَوْعَىٰٓ (and stored it up) to show a sequence of actions.

steps.m: and he gathered (followed by فَأَوْعَىٰٓ - and stored it up)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this action with what came before it. This shows a sequence: first he gathered, then he stored up.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added أ at beginning to make the meaning more intense - changes from simple gathering to excessive hoarding
Added فَ to show this happened after gathering mentioned in previous word (وَجَمَعَ - and he gathered)
Added a long vowel mark to show he hoarded wealth very greedily
This is a special word that emphasizes what comes after it. In this ayah, it makes the statement about mankind stronger, like saying 'Indeed, mankind...' instead of just 'Mankind...'

steps.m: to be social or familiar with others
steps.n: This is the basic meaning that relates to human companionship
Root means 'to be social' - adding vowels turns it into a noun meaning 'humans' (since humans are social creatures)
Adding ان to إِنْس creates the common word for a single human being
Added ال at the beginning to say 'the human' instead of just 'a human'

steps.m: the human being (being acted upon by خُلِقَ - was created)
steps.n: Added a fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of being created
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

Changed to passive voice by changing the vowels to show that the creation happened to someone rather than someone doing the creating. This word describes mankind (الإنسٰن) who is described as anxious (هَلُوعًا).
This describes someone who gets very worried or upset quickly

steps.m: one who panics a lot (subject of خُلِقَ)
steps.n: Added و after second letter and ُ on second letter to show this describes someone who does this action intensely. This word describes how mankind was created in the phrase خُلِقَ هَلُوعًا (was created anxious).

steps.m: as very anxious/panicky (describing how mankind was created)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is describing the state or condition in which mankind was created, completing the meaning of خُلِقَ (was created).
This is the basic word that shows a point in time, like saying 'at that time' or 'when'
Adding ا at the end makes it specifically about future events. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when evil touches someone.
The middle letter س is doubled with shadda (ّ) to show the touching/affecting was strong or intense. This happens because two identical letters (س-س) come together.
The هُ at the end shows who was affected. This word connects to الشَّرُّ (evil/harm) which is doing the affecting.
The doubled ر shows this is very intense or severe harm
Added ٱل to make it specific 'the harmful thing'

steps.m: the harmful thing (subject of مَسَّهُ - touched him)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this word is doing the action in مَسَّهُ (touched him)

steps.m: to panic or be very anxious

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added و after second letter to show this describes someone who does this action frequently

steps.m: very panicky (describing the state of مَسَّهُ ٱلشَّرُّ - when evil touches him)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes how something happens. Here it describes the state of the person when evil touches them.
This is a time word that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it introduces 'when good touches him'

steps.m: and when (introducing the condition مَسَّهُ ٱلْخَيْرُ)
steps.n: The و connects this condition with what came before it in the previous ayah
Added fatha vowels and combined the two س letters with a shadda (doubling mark) to show emphasis
The هُ at the end means 'him' and refers back to الْخَيْرُ (good fortune) in this ayah
Added short vowels (fatha and sukun) to make it a noun meaning 'a good thing' instead of just the root letters

steps.m: the good thing (subject of مَسَّهُ - touched him)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific ('the'), and ُ at the end to show it's doing the action in مَسَّهُ (touched him)
Added و after second letter and changed vowels to show someone who does this action intensely as part of their character

steps.m: extremely withholding (describing how someone acts when good comes to them)
steps.n: The ًا at the end shows this describes how the person acts when الخير (good/wealth) touches them - they become extremely stingy and withholding

steps.m: except (excluding ٱلْمُصَلِّينَ from what came before)
steps.n: This is a basic exception word that excludes something from what was mentioned before it. In this ayah, it excludes ٱلْمُصَلِّينَ (those who pray) from the previous statement.

steps.m: to pray or bow down in worship
steps.n: The root letters convey the basic idea of prayer and bowing down
The root letters get vowels added, with extra emphasis (double letter) in middle to show doing something with more effort. The last letter changes to ى at the end.
Adding مُ at the start turns the verb into a noun meaning 'one who does the action'
Sound masculine plural formed by adding ين
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to specific people who pray (comes after إِلَّا/except in this ayah)

steps.m: the people who pray (object of إِلَّا/except)
steps.n: The َ shows this word is affected by إِلَّا (except) in this ayah
Added ن to make it refer to multiple people or things
Added ال at the beginning and doubled the ذ letter to make it mean 'those who'. This word describes the people who are دَآئِمُونَ (constant/regular) in their prayers.
This is a personal pronoun that refers to a group of people (all male or mixed gender). In this ayah, it shows who is being described as 'constant/regular' (دَآئِمُونَ) in their prayers.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of, about, or concerning something else
Added the alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is just a spelling variation of ى. In this ayah, it shows the connection between being constant (دَآئِمُونَ) and their prayers (صَلَاتِهِمْ)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The root word for 'pray' gets changed to mean 'prayer' by adding an alif after the second letter. The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Added taa marbouta (ة) at the end to make it feminine and specify a single instance of prayer
The word changed to show 'their prayer' by adding هِمْ ('him') at the end, which means 'their' when talking about a group
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from a verb (he continued) to a word describing who does the continuing action. The middle letter changes to make it easier to say.
Makes the word plural to show more than two people who continue
The ون ending shows this describes multiple people (all male or mixed gender), and the ن gets a vowel mark to connect with هُم
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence
Added ال to make it specific
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. The ال changes to ٱلّ with a shadda for pronunciation.
Basic preposition that shows something is inside or part of something else. Here it connects to أَمْوٰلِهِمْ (their wealth) to show what contains the rights mentioned.
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show how to pronounce it correctly
Changed the ي to ى with a small alif (ٓ) on top. This is just a writing style variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Root letters م-و-ل became مَال by changing و to ا (alif) to make the basic word for wealth
Forms broken plural showing multiple types or large amounts of wealth
The alif at the end changed shape and got a small line under it to show it belongs to someone

steps.m: their wealth/properties (referring to those who have a known right in their wealth)
steps.n: Added pronoun suffix to show ownership by multiple people
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled letter makes the meaning stronger and turns it into a noun
Adding the double damma makes it indefinite (a truth vs the truth)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes the word to show what is affected by the knowing, rather than who does the knowing
Adds tanween (double damma) to show this is talking about something general or undefined
Doubling the first letter adds emphasis to show something is very well-known
Changed from root letters to past tense verb 'he asked' by adding vowel marks
Changed the word to show who does the action
Added لِل which combines ل (for/to) and ال (the). The shadda on س comes from combining the ل and س sounds.

steps.m: for the one who asks (connected to والمحروم)
steps.n: Added final kasra to show connection with والمحروم (and the deprived one). The آ is just a writing style for ا.

steps.m: to prevent access or deny something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added مَ at the start and changed the middle to make it mean 'someone who is prevented' instead of 'to prevent'
Added ال to point to specific deprived people

steps.m: and the one who is prevented/denied
steps.n: Added و at start to connect this with السَّآئِلِ (the beggar) mentioned before it

steps.m: and the deprived one (connected to لِّلسَّآئِلِ)
steps.n: Added kasra at end because it's connected to 'for the beggar' earlier in the verse
This is the basic form of the word that connects parts of a sentence by referring back to something mentioned before
Added ال to make it specific
Added ين to show it refers to multiple people. This word is the subject of يُصَدِّقُونَ (they believe/affirm) in this ayah.
Added و at beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. The ال changes to ٱلّ with a shadda when connected.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled د (shadda) intensifies the meaning, showing strong belief or confirmation
Changes it from past to present tense

steps.m: they strongly confirm/believe (referring to those who believe in the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Makes it plural - shows that multiple people are doing the action. This word relates to وَٱلَّذِينَ (and those who) that comes before it in the ayah.
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'day'
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'in/by' and ِ at the end because it's connected to the word الدِّينِ (the judgment/religion) that comes after it

steps.m: to judge, to follow a way of life

steps.n: Adding kasra and connecting letters transforms the root into a noun meaning the result of judgment or following a way

steps.m: the judgment, the way of life
steps.n: Adding ال (the) makes it 'the religion/judgment' instead of just any religion/judgment

steps.m: of the judgment (connected to يَوْمِ - day)
steps.n: Adding kasra at the end shows this word describes or belongs to يَوْمِ (day), making the phrase 'Day of Judgment'
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Added 'the' to make it specific
Changed to plural form to refer to multiple people

steps.m: and those who (who are fearful of their Lord's punishment)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect this to the previous phrase. This describes people who are fearful of their Lord's punishment, as shown in the next part of the verse.
This is a basic pronoun used to refer to a group of males or mixed group. In this ayah, it's connected to مُّشْفِقُونَ (fearful/worried) to show who is fearful.
Added damma (ُ) on ه to show this is the subject form of the pronoun
Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or connects parts of a sentence
Added shadda to show strong connection with next word. This preposition connects with عَذَابِ (punishment) to show what they are fearful of.
Added sukun at the end to show where the word stops
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif to change from the action to the result of the action. This word is connected to رَبِّهِم (their Lord) in this ayah.
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this punishment belongs to their Lord (رَبِّهِم)

steps.m: to care for and develop something/someone to their full potential

steps.n: The shadda (doubled letter) shows this is someone who really takes care of others completely

steps.m: their Lord (subject of مُّشْفِقُونَ - they are fearful)
steps.n: Added هِم at the end to show it belongs to them. The ِ (kasra) helps connect it smoothly. This word is what they are fearful about in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who does this action
Adding shadda (double mark) on م makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: those who are anxiously concerned (subject of هُم)
steps.n: Added ون to make it plural. This word describes 'they' (هُم) in this ayah.

Adding shadda changes this from a conditional word to one that adds emphasis. It makes the statement stronger, like adding an exclamation mark in English. Here it emphasizes that the punishment is definitely not safe from.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: punishment, torment (acted on, owned by ربهم)
steps.n: Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun that describes the action. This word is in the accusative case because it comes after إِنَّ and is possessed by رَبِّهِمْ (their Lord) in a possession relationship.
The fatha at the end shows this word is affected by إِنَّ (indeed), which makes it the object being talked about
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
The shadda (doubling) intensifies the meaning to indicate complete lordship
Added kasra to connect it to the next part
Added هِمْ to show possession. This word is being described by غَيْرُ مَأْمُونٍ (not safe) in this ayah.

steps.m: to be different or other than

steps.n: Basic noun form meaning 'other than' or 'different from'
Adds the ُ mark because this word describes something that comes after it (عَذَابَ رَبِّهِمْ)
This root carries the basic meaning of safety and security

steps.m: made safe/secure (something that is protected from harm)
steps.n: Added مَ at start and made middle letter ا to show this is a thing that has been made safe/protected
Added tanween kasra at the end to show this describes عَذَابَ (punishment) in the phrase 'punishment that is not secure/safe from'
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence
Added the definite article to make it specific
Changed to plural form to refer to multiple people
Added 'and' to connect with previous statement. This word is the subject of حٰفِظُونَ (they guard/protect) in this ayah.
This is a standalone pronoun that shows multiple people (all male or mixed group). In this ayah, it's the subject (doer) of حٰفِظُونَ (guards/protects).
The root meaning changes to refer to a specific body part
Changes the basic action into a noun for a specific body part
Changes the word to show many of something
Adds kasra to connect with next word
Adds 'their' to show who the private parts belong to

steps.m: for their private parts (connected to حٰفِظُونَ)
steps.n: Adds 'for/to' at the start. This connects to حٰفِظُونَ (guards/protects) to show what is being guarded.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from a verb (action) to describe someone who does the action

steps.m: those who protect/guard (plural, referring to believers)
steps.n: Adding ون at the end makes it plural (more than two). The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this form. This describes the believers mentioned earlier in the ayah.
This is an exception particle that shows something is being excluded from a previous statement

steps.m: except (from عَلَىٰٓ أَزْوٰجِهِمْ - upon their spouses)
steps.n: Adding the shadda (ّ) and alif (ا) makes the word stronger and clearer. In this ayah, it's showing an exception - that something is allowed for spouses but not for others.
Basic preposition showing something is physically or metaphorically above or on top of something else
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) which is a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning
Added a small alif (ٓ) above to show this connects to the word after it (أَزْوٰجِهِمْ - their spouses)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it into a noun for one spouse or pair
The word becomes plural by adding أَ at the start and changing the middle to وا, making it mean 'more than one spouse/pair'
The word 'spouses' got a small line on top (ٰ) and a slanted line under (ِ) to show it belongs with the next word
Added هِمْ which means 'their' when referring to a group

steps.m: or (connecting أَزْوٰجِهِمْ with ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمٰنُهُمْ)
steps.n: Basic particle that gives a choice between two options: their spouses OR what their right hands possess

steps.m: or (connecting أَزْوٰجِهِمْ with ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمٰنُهُمْ)
steps.n: Added fatha on أ and sukun on و to show correct pronunciation
This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it links to مَلَكَتْ (possessed/owned) to mean 'what they possessed.' It's like saying 'the things that' they own.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make it past tense
Added ت with sukun at the end to show this is feminine singular past tense. In this ayah, this word describes ما (what/that which) which takes feminine agreement.

steps.m: to be on the right side or be blessed

steps.n: Added vowels to the root letters to make the word mean 'right hand' instead of the action of being on the right
This makes the word plural by changing its pattern. It's an irregular (broken) plural in Arabic.
Changed the middle alif to a longer alif (alif maqsurah) and added a u-sound at the end
Added هُمْ at the end to show possession by a group
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before
Added هُمْ at the end to show this refers to them (the people mentioned before in the ayah)
Basic noun form showing something that is different or other than something else
Added damma at the end to show this word describes مَلُومِينَ (blamed ones) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to passive form by adding مَ at start and putting ُ in middle - shows who/what gets blamed instead of who does the blaming

steps.m: blamed ones (those who are not blamed)
steps.n: Added ين to make it plural - this changes it from 'blamed' to 'blamed ones'. Used with غَيْرُ in this ayah to mean 'not blamed ones'
This is a question word that can also mean 'whoever' when used in conditional sentences. Here it starts a conditional clause about 'whoever seeks beyond that'
The فَ shows this is a result or consequence of what came before. It connects to ٱبْتَغَىٰ (seeks) that comes after it
The ending changes to kasra (ِ) because this word is the subject (doer) of the action 'seeks' that follows it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added ا at start and ت after first root letter to show active, intentional seeking. The word relates to seeking something beyond (وَرَآءَ) 'that' (ذٰلِكَ)
Changed hamza to alif wasl (ٱ) since it's at the start of the word, and added the alif maqsura (ى) at the end because it's a weak-letter verb
Adds alif to show this is a place/location word
Added hamza at the end and lengthened the alif to complete the word. This word relates to ذٰلِكَ (that) in the ayah, meaning 'beyond that'.
This is the basic demonstrative word used to point to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show this points to something far away instead of nearby
Adding لِ makes the word point to something even farther away
Adding كَ makes this word mean 'that over there' - it's like pointing to something far away
This is a plural demonstrative pronoun
Added ك at the end to show we are addressing or referring to them directly
Added فَ at beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before
This is the basic form of the third person masculine plural pronoun, used to refer to a group of males or mixed group
Added damma vowel marks to show this is the subject form of the pronoun. This pronoun is the subject of ٱلْعَادُونَ (the transgressors) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the word to show who does the action instead of the action itself
Added ون to show we're talking about many people who do this

steps.m: the ones who go beyond limits (subject of هُمُ)
steps.n: Added ال to point to specific transgressors. This word follows هُمُ (they are) in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence
Added the definite article to make it specific
Changed to plural form to show it refers to multiple people

steps.m: and those who (who guard their trusts and promises)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect with previous statement. This introduces people who protect their trusts and promises.
This is a basic pronoun that refers to a group of males or mixed group
Added damma (ُ) on ه and sukun (ْ) on م to show correct pronunciation. This pronoun is the subject of رٰعُونَ (they observe/guard)

steps.m: a trust (something given to keep safe)
steps.n: The root أ-م-ن about trust becomes أَمَانَة (amaanah) meaning 'something given to keep safe'. The ة makes it a noun for one specific trust.
Makes it plural to show multiple trusts/responsibilities
Adds kasra to indicate genitive case

steps.m: their trusts (things given to them to keep safe)
steps.n: Adding هم makes it possessive - 'their trusts'

steps.m: to/regarding their trusts (things they are responsible for keeping safe)
steps.n: Adds لِ to show this word connects to رٰعُونَ (observers/keepers) - they are observers of their trusts

steps.m: to make a covenant or promise

steps.n: Root letters put together with vowels to make the noun 'promise'
Added kasra to show possession
Added pronoun 'their'
Added conjunction 'and' to connect with previous phrase. This word is part of what they observe (رٰعُونَ) in the ayah.

steps.m: one who watches over and protects
steps.n: Added ا after ر and vowels to show who does the action

steps.m: those who watch over and protect
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show it's talking about many people. The ي drops out because it can't stay with ون

steps.m: those who watch over and protect (subject of هُمْ)
steps.n: The alif (ا) is written as a small line above the ر, and we add a fatha (َ) at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence
Added ال to make it definite
Changed to plural form to show it refers to multiple people
Added 'and' to connect this phrase with what came before, and added proper vowel marks
This is a standalone pronoun that means 'they' for a group of males or mixed group. In this ayah, it shows who is 'upholding' (قَآئِمُونَ) their testimonies.
Changed the root into a noun by adding alif after second letter and ة at end to mean 'the act of testifying'
Changes from one testimony to multiple testimonies by adding ات at the end
Adds the possessive pronoun 'their'
Added preposition bi- meaning 'with'. The word relates to قَآئِمُونَ (they uphold/maintain) showing how they are upholding their testimonies.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he stood) to describe someone who does the standing
Changes from 'one who stands' to 'many people who stand' by adding ون at the end
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence
Added ال to make it definite
Changed to plural form by adding ين

steps.m: and those who (who maintain their prayers)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect with previous statement. This word connects to يُحَافِظُونَ (they maintain/guard) in the ayah.
This is a personal pronoun used for a group of males or mixed gender. In this ayah, it's the subject of the verb يُحَافِظُونَ (they guard/maintain).
Basic form of the preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word - fatha (َ) on ع and ل
Added alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) which is a small mark above the ى to show it's pronounced as a long 'aa' sound
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from verb 'to pray' into the noun 'prayer' by adding alif and ta marbuta
Added -i ending to show this prayer belongs to someone
Adds 'their' to show the prayer belongs to them
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding alif after first letter shows the action is done regularly or with effort
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word refers to how the believers consistently maintain their prayers (صَلَاتِهِمْ)
This is the basic form of the word that means 'those'
Adding ئِ shows we're pointing to something far away, like saying 'over there' in English

steps.m: those (far away, pointing to مُّكْرَمُونَ - honored ones)
steps.n: Adding كَ makes it point specifically to the honored ones (مُّكْرَمُونَ) mentioned in the next part of the verse

steps.m: basic concept of being inside or within something
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. It's one of the most common words in Arabic
Added kasra (ِ) to show it's a preposition. In this ayah, it shows that 'those' (أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ) are located in 'gardens' (جَنَّاتٍ).
The ي at the end is written as ى when it's at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters and added the shadda to show emphasis on covering
Adding ة changes it from 'covering' to mean the place that is covered with trees
Makes the word plural to show multiple gardens (in في جَنَّاتٍ - in gardens)
The ٍ shows this comes after the word في (in) in the phrase في جَنَّاتٍ (in gardens)
Added vowels to make it mean 'he was' noble/honorable in the past
Changed to passive participle form showing someone receiving honor
Added plural ending to show multiple people being honored
Added -a sound at end to show this is the subject form
Starting with the question word ما (what)
Added لِ (with/for) to express the meaning of possession or association

steps.m: Then what is with (the disbelievers who are rushing towards you)
steps.n: Added فَ (then/so) to connect this phrase with the previous context, showing sequence or result
Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it a specific relative pronoun. This word connects descriptions to people or things, like saying 'the one who' in English.
Added ين at the end to make it refer to multiple people. This word connects to كَفَرُوا۟ (they disbelieved) in this ayah, describing who did the action.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: they rejected (subject of مُهْطِعِينَ - rushing forward)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. This word describes the disbelievers who are rushing forward (مُهْطِعِينَ) in the verse.
Adding vowels changes this into a preposition meaning 'before'

steps.m: before you (facing مُهْطِعِينَ - those rushing headlong)
steps.n: Added كَ to show this is happening in your direction. This word relates to مُهْطِعِينَ (those rushing headlong) in the next part of the ayah, showing where they are rushing.

steps.m: to rush forward anxiously with neck stretched out

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: one who is rushing forward anxiously
steps.n: Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone doing the action

steps.m: ones who are rushing forward anxiously (describing كَفَرُوا۟)
steps.n: Added ين to show this describes multiple people. This word describes those who disbelieved (كَفَرُوا۟) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows movement away from something or discussing a topic
Added kasra at the end to connect it with the next word ٱلْيَمِينِ (the right side). This shows it's working with that word to tell us what's being moved away from.
Added vowels and the ي after the second root letter to make it a noun meaning 'right side'

steps.m: the right side (connected to عَنِ meaning 'from/on')
steps.n: Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific ('the right side') and ِ at the end because it follows the word عَنِ (meaning 'from/on')
Base preposition meaning 'away from'. In this ayah, it works with عِزِينَ to describe how people are scattered away from different directions.
Adds 'and' to connect it to the previous phrase عَنِ ٱلْيَمِينِ (away from the right), showing people are scattered in multiple directions

steps.m: to gather, to include on the left side

steps.n: Changes from basic root to noun pattern فِعَال indicating direction/location
The kasra ending shows this word is connected to عَنِ (meaning 'from/on')
Adds emphasis through gemination of initial letter
Adds 'the' (ال) to make it specifically 'the left side', connected to عَنِ (meaning 'from/on')

steps.m: to gather or separate into groups

steps.n: Changes verb into noun meaning 'a group'
Changes from عِزَو to عِزِين by adding ين at the end to show many separate groups (like breaking into teams)
Adds َ to show the word is being used as an object in the sentence
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on middle letter, and ُ at end

steps.m: does he eagerly want? (referring to entering Paradise)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question. This is asking about whether each person hopes to enter Paradise, mentioned in the next part of the verse.
Root 'k-l-l' becomes 'kull' meaning 'all/every' - the shadda makes it stronger like saying 'absolutely all'
Added final damma to show this word is the subject of يَطْمَعُ (hopes/desires). In the ayah, it means 'Does every person among them hope to enter a garden of pleasure?'
Root letters combine to make the basic word for 'person'
Added 'i' sound at start and end to show 'a person' in possessive form
This word is being described by كُلُّ (each) in the ayah, showing we're talking about any/every person
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added emphasis mark (shadda) on م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel (أَيَطْمَعُ)

steps.m: from them (connected to كُلُّ ٱمْرِئٍ - every person)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being referred to - in this case referring back to 'every person' mentioned earlier

steps.m: that (connects to يُدْخَلَ - he will enter)
steps.n: This particle connects two parts of a sentence and shows what someone hopes or expects will happen. Here it connects 'Does each one of them hope' with 'he will enter gardens of pleasure'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show this is happening to something rather than someone doing it

steps.m: to be entered (that he will be made to enter جَنَّةَ نَعِيمٍ)
steps.n: Added يُ at the beginning to show this will happen in the future, and changed the middle vowel to sukun. The final fatha shows this is the result of hoping/wanting (connected to أَيَطْمَعُ - does he hope/expect)

steps.m: to cover or conceal (like how a garden is an enclosed space)

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this is a basic past-tense verb
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning by doubling the middle letter
Added ة to make it a noun meaning 'a single enclosed space' which came to mean 'garden'
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being entered in the phrase 'to enter garden'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (being happy) to a noun meaning intense happiness/bliss
The tanween at the end shows this word describes what kind of garden (جَنَّةَ) it is - a garden of perfect bliss
This is the basic form of the word that means 'no'
Adding shadda makes the word stronger

steps.m: No indeed! (strongly rejecting what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Adding the long alif at the end makes this a complete phrase that strongly rejects something
This is a particle that makes what comes after it more emphatic
Added 'ا' at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This connects to the next word خَلَقْنٰهُم (we created them).

steps.m: to create, form, or shape something

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding sukun (stop mark) on last letter shows where to pause when reading
Adding نا shows multiple people did the action together
Adding هم shows who received the action (them)
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added ما which means 'what' to specify what we're talking about
The ن combines with م of ما, creating a doubled م (shown by ّ). This is just a pronunciation change that doesn't affect the meaning. This word is what they know about in يَعْلَمُونَ (they know).
Connected the root letters together with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
The يَ at the start shows it's happening now
Adding ون shows this is about multiple people (they) rather than just one person (he)
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation
Extended the alif for emphasis in pronunciation

steps.m: Then not/So not (connects to أُقْسِمُ - I swear)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this negation to what follows. Here it connects to the oath that follows (I swear)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb

steps.m: I swear (negated by لَا before it)
steps.n: Added أُ at start to show 'I' am doing it, changed vowels to show it's happening now, and added ُ at end to complete the present tense form. This word is negated by لَا before it in the ayah.
The doubled ب (shown by ّ) makes this mean 'one who does this action a lot' - so a lord is one who constantly nurtures and develops things
The ِ (kasra) at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it, like saying 'Lord of...'
Added بِ at the beginning to show this lord is being sworn by in the oath (أُقْسِمُ). This word is connected to 'the east and west' that comes after it.

steps.m: to shine/rise (like the sun in the east)

steps.n: Added مَ at the start and ِ in the middle to make this word mean 'the place where something happens'
Added ا after ر to make the word plural, showing multiple eastern places

steps.m: the places where the sun rises (connected to رَبِّ - Lord of)
steps.n: Added 'al' (ٱلْ) at start to mean 'the' and a kasra (ِ) at end because it's connected to 'Lord of' (رَبِّ)
Adding مَ at the start makes it mean 'place where something happens'
Adding ا makes it plural - like how we say 'schools' instead of 'school'
Added ال to make it specific
Added وَ (and) at start and ِ at end to show it belongs to رَبِّ (Lord)
This is an emphatic particle that adds strength to what follows, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly' in English
Added نا at the end to show this refers to 'we'. This word connects to لَقٰدِرُونَ (capable) to mean 'indeed we are capable'
The vowels َ and ِ make this a verb about having an ability or quality
Changed from verb 'to be able' to noun 'one who is able' by adding alif
Added لَ to emphasize that we are truly/indeed capable
Added plural ending ون to show 'we' are doing the action
This is a common preposition that shows a relationship where one thing is above or has power over another.
Added an alif that shows this is a long 'aa' sound at the end. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: upon/over (connected to نُّبَدِّلَ - we can exchange)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ٓ) to show this word connects to the next word نُّبَدِّلَ (we can exchange).

steps.m: that (introduces what is possible/able to happen with نُّبَدِّلَ)
steps.n: This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it comes before the verb نُّبَدِّلَ (we replace/substitute) to show what is possible or able to happen.
The doubled middle letter shows this is done thoroughly or with intensity
The نُ at the start shows 'we' are doing the action. The word follows أَن (that/to) which makes it show purpose or possibility.
The ن gets a shadda for pronunciation when it follows certain words
Changes from verb 'to be good' into the noun 'good'

steps.m: better ones (object of نُّبَدِّلَ - we will replace)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this word is receiving the action of replacement. In the phrase 'We will replace them with better ones', this word is what they will be replaced with.
This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or is being compared to something else
Adding the shadda (ّ) makes the م sound stronger, which happens when certain words come before مِنْ

steps.m: from them/than them (خَيْرًا - better than them)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being referred to. In this ayah, it's comparing something that's better (خَيْرًا) than them.
Basic particle ما that can mean either 'not' (negation) or 'what' (question word). In this ayah, it means 'not' because it's being used with نَحْنُ بِمَسْبُوقِينَ (we are not to be outdone)

steps.m: and not (we are not to be outdone)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before. This connects the negation to the previous statement about replacing them with better people.
This is the basic form of the pronoun meaning 'we' - used when multiple people are speaking about themselves

steps.m: we (as the subject of نُّبَدِّلَ - we replace)
steps.n: Added the ُ (damma) at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence. Here it's the subject of the verb نُّبَدِّلَ (we replace).
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from 'he raced' to 'someone who gets raced past' - like going from runner to the one being run past
Made it plural to show it's talking about multiple people

steps.m: among those who are outpaced (connected to نَحْنُ - we)
steps.n: Added بِ to show this describes being among this group. The word relates to نَحْنُ (we) in the ayah.
Forms the basic command form by using fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter. The initial و from the root is dropped in commands.

steps.m: so leave! (connected to what came before)
steps.n: The prefix فَ connects this command to the previous context, showing it's a result or response

steps.m: so leave them! (let them continue in what they're doing, referring to those who are playing and amusing themselves)
steps.n: The word changed from 'leave!' to 'leave them!' by adding هُمْ (them) at the end, showing who is being left
Connected the root letters with basic vowels to form verbal noun
Added present tense prefix ي and vowels to create singular present form

steps.m: they plunge into (connects to وَيَلْعَبُوا۟ - and they play)
steps.n: The وا shows multiple people doing the action. This verb is connected to وَيَلْعَبُوا۟ (and they play) in the ayah, showing these actions happening together.
Basic past tense verb showing someone did this action
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from singular to plural by adding وا at the end

steps.m: and they play (while others يَخُوضُوا۟ - dive into vain discourse)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this action with the previous verb يَخُوضُوا۟ (dive into vain discourse)
This is a particle that means 'until' or 'up to'. It doesn't come from a root since it's a basic building block of Arabic.
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning and fatha to help pronunciation
Added alif maqsura (ى with two dots) at the end to show this connects to what comes after it. Here it connects to يُلٰقُوا۟ (they meet/encounter).

steps.m: to meet, encounter, or come face to face with

steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb
Added يُ at start to show present/future tense, and ا after first root letter to show ongoing action
Added وا to show plural 'they', and changed vowels due to being in a subjunctive mood after حَتَّىٰ (until). The long ا is written with ٰ above the letter.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'day'

steps.m: their day (what they will meet in يُلٰقُوا)
steps.n: Added هُمُ at the end to show the day belongs to them. This day is what they will meet/encounter according to the verb يُلٰقُوا in the ayah.
This is the base form of the relative pronoun used to connect clauses, like 'which' or 'who' in English

steps.m: who/which/that (connects to يُوعَدُونَ - they are promised)
steps.n: Added ال with shadda to make it a specific relative pronoun. This word connects 'their day' to 'they are promised' in the next word.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are being promised. This refers to the same group that is mentioned in يَخُوضُوا (they indulge) in the ayah.

steps.m: on the Day (when يَخْرُجُونَ they will emerge)
steps.n: Connected the letters with vowel marks to form the word for 'day'. This word is setting up when the action of emerging (يَخْرُجُونَ) will happen.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The يَ at the start shows it's happening now or in the future. The vowel pattern changes to show ongoing action.

steps.m: they go out/exit (referring to the disbelievers emerging from their graves on the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: The ون ending shows multiple males are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to disbelievers emerging from their graves quickly on the Day of Judgment, as mentioned in the following words مِنَ ٱلْأَجْدَاثِ (from the graves)
Basic preposition showing starting point or source
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word الْأَجْدَاثِ (the graves). Together they mean 'from the graves'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a real word
Changed to plural form by adding أ at start and ا after second root letter
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it follows مِنَ (from)
Added vowels (fatha and damma) to make it mean 'he was quick' in the past
Changed from a verb (he was quick) to plural noun (quick ones) by adding an alif and changing the vowels

steps.m: quickly (describing how they emerge from graves)
steps.n: Added tanween fathatain to make it describe how the action يَخْرُجُونَ (they emerge) happens
This is a basic comparison particle that shows similarity
Adding أَنَّ changes 'like' to 'as if', making it introduce a comparison

steps.m: as if they (are rushing toward نُصُبٍ)
steps.n: The هُمْ suffix refers to the group of people being described in the ayah who are rushing toward نُصُبٍ (idols/altars)
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it shows where they are rushing to (نُصُبٍ meaning 'stone altars').
The alif at the end is written with a small mark above it (called an alif maqsurah). This is just a writing variation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to set up or establish something

steps.n: The damma vowels (ُ) change this from a verb into a plural noun meaning multiple idols
Added two dots under the last letter to show it's talking about any one idol that they are rushing to
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. The و of the root stays silent after يُ.
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, they are rushing towards نُصُبٍ (idols/altars).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes the verb into a word describing someone or something that has this quality. This word is describing their eyes (أَبْصٰر) in the verse.
Makes the word feminine to match with the feminine word it's describing (eyes/أَبْصٰر)
Adds tanween (double fatha) to show this is describing their eyes (أَبْصٰر) in the sentence

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes

steps.n: Changes the word from an action (seeing) into a plural noun (eyes/sights). The alif after ص makes it plural, while the أ at the beginning helps form this special plural pattern.
The ُ (damma) shows this word is doing the action in تَرْهَقُهُمْ (covers them)
Adds هُمْ at the end to show these eyes belong to them. This word is the subject of تَرْهَقُهُمْ (covers them) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added damma at the end to show present tense nominative case

steps.m: she/it overwhelms them (referring to the disbelievers)
steps.n: Adds pronoun 'them' as the object being overwhelmed
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to show strong humiliation
Changed to a noun showing the state of being humiliated
Adding the two damma marks makes it 'a humiliation' instead of 'the humiliation'
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is replaced with a long alif (ٰ) to extend the pointing distance
Adding لِ changes it to point to something far away

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (refers to ٱلْيَوْمُ - the Day)
steps.n: Adding كَ completes the word. In this ayah, it points to 'the Day' (ٱلْيَوْمُ) mentioned right after it.
Basic noun meaning 'day' with short vowels added (fatHa and sukoon). Comes after 'that' in the sentence.

steps.m: the day (that is being referred to by ذٰلِكَ)
steps.n: ٱلْ makes it specific, ُ shows it's doing something
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence

steps.m: the one who/which/that (refers to يَوْمُ - the Day)
steps.n: Added ال with shadda to make it definite and connect it specifically to something mentioned before (in this case, the Day that was promised)
Adding the fatha (َ) makes it a past tense verb
Adding another fatha (َ) completes the past tense verb pattern
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action. This word is the subject of يُوعَدُونَ (they are promised) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice present tense - shows receiving the promise/warning rather than giving it

steps.m: they are being promised/warned (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in أَبْصٰرُهُمْ)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are receiving the promise/warning. This refers back to the disbelievers whose eyes are mentioned earlier in the verse.
